write_file({"contents": "// Package language_tiers classifies programming languages into tiers\n// that control which certification features apply. TierNative languages\n// enjoy full certification support; TierGeneric languages receive a\n// reduced set of checks.\npackage language_tiers\n\nimport \"strings\"\n\n// Tier represents the certification tier for a programming language.\ntype Tier int\n\nconst (\n\t// TierGeneric is the default tier for languages without native\n\t// certification support. Only universal checks apply.\n\tTierGeneric Tier = 0\n\n\t// TierNative is the tier for languages with full native\n\t// certification support, including language-specific rules.\n\tTierNative Tier = 1\n)\n\n// TierForLanguage returns the certification [Tier] for the given\n// language identifier. The match is case-insensitive. Languages with\n// native support return [TierNative]; all others return [TierGeneric].\nfunc TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier {\n\tswitch {\n\tcase strings.EqualFold(lang, \"go\"):\n\t\treturn TierNative\n\tcase strings.EqualFold(lang, \"typescript\"):\n\t\treturn TierNative\n\tcase strings.EqualFold(lang, \"python\"):\n\t\treturn TierNative\n\tcase strings.EqualFold(lang, \"rust\"):\n\t\treturn TierNative\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn TierGeneric\n\t}\n}\n", "path": "internal/language_tiers/tiers.go"}) → wrote internal/language_tiers/tiers.go (1198 bytes)
